data.requirement: Link customer demographics (age, gender, senior status) with churn status (churn_label) and service attributes (contract type, internet type) through customer_id to enable multi-table analysis.
data.reason: Original requirement mixes valid table linking with non-existent 'income' factor from user's goal. Database contains no income data - adjustment needed to focus on actual demographic columns (age/senior/gender)

data.requirement: Calculate aggregated churn rates across demographic segments (age bins, gender groups) and service categories (contract types). Identify segments with churn rates statistically different from population mean using z-score >2.
data.reason: Original 1.5x threshold is arbitrary. Statistical significance testing better serves outlier identification while removing inaccurate income references

visualization.requirement: Faceted bar charts showing churn rates across two dimensions at a time (e.g., age group x internet_type) with interactive dimension selector.
visualization.reason: 3D heatmaps would become unreadable with 20+ internet_type/contract combinations. Faceting maintains readability while enabling multi-factor analysis

visualization.requirement: Stacked area charts showing churn distribution across tenure cohorts, with interactive time window selection.
visualization.reason: Horizon charts are too specialized for general users. Stacked areas better show tenure-based patterns while aligning with allowed chart types

interaction.requirement: Click-driven bar chart sorting instead of hover, showing top 10 churn reasons for selected demographic/service segments.
interaction.reason: Hover-based sorting creates unintended view changes. Explicit click interactions maintain user control and prevent visual distraction
